title: GeoDE: A Geographically Diverse Evaluation Dataset for Object Recognition
main_finding: Introduces GeoDE as a geographically diverse object recognition evaluation dataset.
limitation: Dataset-specific claims should be drawn from the paper, not memory.
how_it_supports_this_paper: Provides direct dataset motivation and region-balanced evaluation context.

title: The Dollar Street Dataset: Images Representing the Geographic and Socioeconomic Diversity of the World
topic_group: Dollar Street and socioeconomic visual diversity
main_finding: Introduces a supervised household-object image dataset with region, country, and income metadata.
limitation: Local subset/split statistics must be verified separately.
how_it_supports_this_paper: Supports dataset choice and socioeconomic/geographic diversity motivation.

title: Does Object Recognition Work for Everyone?
method: empirical evaluation across countries and income
main_finding: Reports lower object-recognition performance for some non-Western and lower-income contexts in Dollar Street.
limitation: Uses different models and evaluation setup than this paper.
how_it_supports_this_paper: Provides closest prior motivation for regional object-recognition disparity.

title: Unbiased Look at Dataset Bias
main_finding: Shows that recognition datasets can contain dataset-specific biases affecting generalization.
limitation: Older pre-deep-learning era; use for conceptual framing.
how_it_supports_this_paper: Frames why performance on one dataset may not transfer cleanly.

title: REVISE: A Tool for Measuring and Mitigating Bias in Visual Datasets
main_finding: Provides methods for surfacing object, person, and geography-related visual dataset biases.
limitation: Tool is for dataset auditing, not this exact evaluation pipeline.
how_it_supports_this_paper: Supports geography-aware dataset bias framing.

title: ImageNet: A Large-Scale Hierarchical Image Database
main_finding: Introduces ImageNet as a large-scale hierarchical image dataset.
limitation: Does not address this paper's regional evaluation directly.
how_it_supports_this_paper: Supports explanation of pretrained ImageNet setting.

title: Do ImageNet Classifiers Generalize to ImageNet?
main_finding: Finds accuracy drops on newly collected ImageNet-like test sets while preserving rank trends.
limitation: Does not evaluate GeoDE or Dollar Street.
how_it_supports_this_paper: Supports caution about benchmark overreliance and aggregate accuracy.

title: Measuring Robustness to Natural Distribution Shifts in Image Classification
main_finding: Shows natural distribution shifts remain challenging for image classifiers.
limitation: Shift types differ from regional shifts in this paper.
how_it_supports_this_paper: Supports distribution-shift framing for regional evaluation.

title: WILDS: A Benchmark of in-the-Wild Distribution Shifts
main_finding: Frames real-world distribution shifts and standardized evaluation.
limitation: Broad benchmark, not specific to GeoDE or Dollar Street.
how_it_supports_this_paper: Supports motivation for evaluation across real-world subpopulations.

title: In Search of Lost Domain Generalization
main_finding: Emphasizes reproducible and carefully controlled domain generalization evaluation.
limitation: This paper does not train domain-generalization algorithms.
how_it_supports_this_paper: Supports cautious evaluation design and reproducibility.

title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision
main_finding: Introduces CLIP and zero-shot transfer with natural language supervision.
limitation: Does not directly establish regional fairness on this paper's datasets.
how_it_supports_this_paper: Supports model family description for CLIP.

title: Data Determines Distributional Robustness in Contrastive Language Image Pre-training (CLIP)
main_finding: Suggests CLIP robustness is strongly associated with pretraining data distribution.
limitation: Claims should not be transferred causally to this paper without evidence.
how_it_supports_this_paper: Supports cautious discussion of vision-language robustness.

title: Sigmoid Loss for Language Image Pre-Training
main_finding: Introduces SigLIP training objective and scaling behavior.
limitation: Not a regional-bias evaluation paper.
how_it_supports_this_paper: Supports model family description for SigLIP.

title: DINOv2: Learning Robust Visual Features without Supervision
main_finding: Introduces DINOv2 visual features trained without labels on curated data.
limitation: Robustness claims must be tied to the original benchmarks.
how_it_supports_this_paper: Supports self-supervised model-family framing.

title: Masked Autoencoders Are Scalable Vision Learners
main_finding: Introduces MAE as scalable masked image modeling for vision.
limitation: Does not directly address regional bias.
how_it_supports_this_paper: Supports model family description for MAE.

title: A ConvNet for the 2020s
main_finding: Introduces ConvNeXt as a modernized ConvNet family.
limitation: Architecture paper, not regional robustness evidence.
how_it_supports_this_paper: Supports model family description for ConvNeXt.

title: Swin Transformer: Hierarchical Vision Transformer using Shifted Windows
main_finding: Introduces Swin Transformer as a hierarchical vision backbone.
limitation: Architecture paper, not regional robustness evidence.
how_it_supports_this_paper: Supports model family description for Swin.

title: Training Data-Efficient Image Transformers and Distillation through Attention
main_finding: Introduces data-efficient ImageNet training for vision transformers.
limitation: Architecture/training paper, not regional robustness evidence.
how_it_supports_this_paper: Supports model family description for DeiT.

title: MaxViT: Multi-Axis Vision Transformer
main_finding: Introduces MaxViT with blocked local and dilated global attention.
limitation: Architecture paper, not regional robustness evidence.
how_it_supports_this_paper: Supports model family description for MaxViT.

title: Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition
main_finding: Introduces residual learning for deep CNNs.
limitation: Architecture paper, not regional robustness evidence.
how_it_supports_this_paper: Supports model family description for ResNet.

title: ObjectNet: A Large-Scale Bias-Controlled Dataset for Pushing the Limits of Object Recognition Models
main_finding: Provides an object-recognition benchmark controlling background, rotation, and viewpoint.
limitation: Not geographic or socioeconomic; use as broader robustness context.
how_it_supports_this_paper: Supports evaluation beyond standard aggregate accuracy.

title: Distributionally Robust Neural Networks for Group Shifts: On the Importance of Regularization for Worst-Case Generalization
main_finding: Shows average accuracy can mask poor performance on atypical groups.
limitation: Method paper with different datasets; use only for worst-group framing.
how_it_supports_this_paper: Supports discussion of worst-region accuracy as a complementary metric.
